title: Train a within-lap ranker, then beta-calibrate and blend
summary: At each lap only a few cars are plausible pit candidates, so relative urgency within the same lap may be easier to learn than absolute probability.
rationale: At each lap only a few cars are plausible pit candidates, so relative urgency within the same lap may be easier to learn than absolute probability. Learning-to-rank is a common adjacent pattern when leaderboard metric is rank-based and positives are sparse within natural query groups.
implementation_hint: Define query groups as Year×Race×LapNumber and train an XGBoost ranker with positive labels for drivers who pit next lap. Use the ranker score as an additional feature or specialist model; convert OOF scores to probabilities with held-out beta calibration before blending.
expected_effect: Should improve local ordering among contemporaneous drivers, complementing the baseline’s global probability estimates and potentially lifting ROC AUC after blending.
risk: Rank objectives optimize within-group ordering, not calibrated probabilities, so post-processing is required. Groups with zero positives or unusual lap counts need careful handling.
